entityDescription.mainTitle: 2023 22nd International Conference on Solid-State Sensors, Actuators and Microsystems (Transducers)
Dual-Frequency Aluminum Scandium Nitride Piezoelectric Microphones with Wide Bandwidth, Large Dynamic Range, and High Sensitivity for Wind Tunnel Testing
entityDescription.abstract: A dual-frequency piezoelectric micro-electro-mechanical system (MEMS) microphone based on highly doped aluminum scandium nitride (AlSc30%N) thin films have been developed for wind tunnel testing with strict requirements of wide bandwidth, large dynamic range, and high sensitivity. Compared to the MEMS sample based on lead zirconate titanate (PZT), the new AlSc30%N MEMS sample has much higher sensitivity and requires no DC bias. The preliminary aeroacoustic tests, conducted on the BACCHUS test bench of Airbus, show that the sensitivity and signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) is 3.2 mV/Pa and 60.2 dB, and the upper limits of frequency range and dynamic range >55 kHz and >147 dB SPL. The overall performance is better than the state of art microphones for aeroacoustic measurements.

entityDescription.mainTitle: A Research Agenda for Law, Finance and the Environment
Reimagining Corporate Purpose for a Safe and Just World
entityDescription.abstract:
After decades of a rather instrumental and narrow focus on business, with the dominant view identifying the corporation with its shareholders, we are seeing a reawakening of the debate on corporate purpose. However, the debate remains constrained by the Anglo-American shareholder vs stakeholder dichotomy.

In our approach to corporate purpose, we draw on over a decade of multi-jurisdictional comparative corporate law analyses. We posit that companies as ‘creatures of national law’ cannot be understood through an analysis that does not engage with the rich variety of corporate law approaches and corporate law theories. Further, in our research aiming to contribute to sustainability, we undertake interdisciplinary analyses, drawing on legal theory, law and economics, and sustainability science and research. That gives us a basis for reimagining corporate purpose for a safe and just world, in a way that transcends limiting dichotomies and national differences.
tags: European Union; Comparative law; Company law; Legal theory; Corporate boards; Corporate sustainability

entityDescription.mainTitle: A Nordic Perspective on Teacher Well-being in Educational Contexts
'We Go as Far as We Can Without Losing Sleep Over It': Self-Care Among Vocational Teachers in Schools in Disadvantaged Areas
entityDescription.abstract:
ABSTRACT
In the face of rising teacher attrition and burnout in the Nordic countries, it is essential to explore and implement strategies for teacher well-being and self-care to maintain a committed teacher workforce. This article explores self-care strategies employed by vocational teachers in schools located in disadvantaged areas of Oslo. These teachers navigate the tension between providing close support to a socially disadvantaged and vulnerable group of students while also taking care of their own well-being. The study was guided by the following research question: What strategies for self-care do vocational teachers in schools in disadvantaged areas use to maintain their own well-being? We draw on individual in-depth interviews with eight vocational teachers who, in the eyes of their students, succeed in building relationships with their students. The findings suggest that teachers utilise three main strategies: maintaining a sense of clarity in their role as teachers, sharing responsibilities and experiencing joy and pride in their work. The study highlights the importance of teachers’ self-reflection combined with social support. Additionally, it underscores the necessity of incorporating self-care into teacher education, emphasising that schools must actively promote teacher well-being through resources and supportive school cultures.

entityDescription.mainTitle: Media, Dissidence and the War in Ukraine | Tabe Bergman, Jesse Owen He
The moderated war in Ukraine: Twitter, Elon Musk, and the role of private platforms in war coverage
entityDescription.abstract: This chapter investigates the impact of media ownership on the dissemination of war information, using the Russia-Ukraine conflict and Twitter as a case study. Bridging literatures on media ownership and war journalism, the chapter argues that social media platforms introduce a new paradigm of wartime censorship. Utilizing real-time data capture, tweets from the 1,000 most visible accounts during the conflict were analysed at three checkpoints surrounding Twitter’s change of ownership. Findings indicate that Elon Musk’s acquisition overall sustained pre-existing practices of account suspension among these influential accounts but also introduced nuanced shifts in content moderation, including unexpected means of content restriction discovered during data analysis. It is argued that these changes challenge traditional distinctions between editorial decision-making and censorship. The chapter extends existing frameworks of war and media by incorporating social media platforms as key influencers in war narratives and provides empirical insights into the effects of ownership change on war information.

entityDescription.mainTitle: Lokalhistoriewiki.no
Clara Ambjørnsen (1856-1920) – lokalhistoriewiki.no
entityDescription.abstract:
Clara Ambjørnsen ble født i Christiania 28. september 1856 og døde i samme by 15. januar 1920. Clara er kjent som dyktig lærerinne, ivrig fotograf og kunstmaler. Clara var datter av Kjøpmann Carl Ludvig Ambjørnsen (f.1827) og Hulda Christine Ambjørnsen (f. Fougner i 1839). Hun var søster av Lærerinde Anna Christina Christine Blixenkrone Fle Chrestene Jacobine Castberg Ambjørnsen (1854-1943), Anna Ambjørnsen (f.1854), Døvstummelærerinde Jacobine Marga Christine Menga Jakobine Ambjørnsen (f.1862), Hans Eduard Caspary Ambjørnsen (f.1863), Hulda Christine Ambjørnsen (f.1868), Jørgen Aarøe Ambjørnsen (f.1871) og Overrettssagfører Carl Aksel Ludvig Ambjørnsen Hagen Ambjörnsen (f.1878). Hun bodde sine første år i Josephinesgade 3 i Christiania og flyttet til Uranienborgveien 3 i samme by 1885. Hun bodde på denne siste adressen resten av sitt liv. Clara arbeidet som lærer hele sitt liv, var ivrig fotograf og dyktig maler. Den 6. november 1874 sendte Kjøpmand William Schmidt et registrert telegram til kurator for Clara Ambjørnsen. Det er dokumentasjon på hennes kunstneriske produksjon og gjelder trolig hennes malerier.

Hun arbeidet som lærer frem til september 1919. På hennes siste kjente timeplan fra september 1919, limt inn i lokket på hennes pennal, står det at hun i den måneden alene underviste i geografi, tabellregning, norsk stil, skriving, historie, salmeforklaring, naturhistorie, håndarbeid, regning, tysk, lesing, bibelhistorie, geografisk kartlegging, tegning og tysk stil. Hun gikk bort under fire måneder senere i januar 1920. Pennalet på bildet inneholder alt fra pennesplittholdere i bakelitt, pennesplitter (spiss og bred) grifler til bruk på skifertavle, blyanter, viskelær, voks, fargeblyanter, malerpensler og en lærerindefløyte til å kalle inn elevene fra friminutt. Det er tydelig at Clara Ambjørnsen var en måteholden dame som brukte sine skriveredskaper til de knapt kunne holdes.
